feature_completeness.reason: All features implemented, including full query parser, structured queries, BM25, exact adjacency/proximity phrase queries, fuzzy, boosts, and snippets.

correctness_edge_cases.reason: Handles all edge cases gracefully, tested extensively, and processes removing/updating documents correctly.

ranking_quality.reason: Correct BM25 formula implementation with proximity bonus for phrases, tied by deterministic document ID asc.

type_safety_api_design.reason: Strict type safety, generic over Doc, cleanly exported structured AST and options types.

performance.reason: Single term p50 ~1.2ms, slightly above target 500µs, but very strong fuzzy performance (p50 ~7ms) despite no external index.

test_evidence.reason: Extensive testing (62 tests), hitting every documented edge case and requirement explicitly.

verification_artifacts.reason: Excellent detailed execution and decision logs capturing real complexity considerations and bugfixes.

code_quality_minimality.reason: Well structured into specific modules (parser, engine, index, snippet), highly readable and zero-dependency.

readme_quality.reason: Comprehensive README with proper type signatures, limitations, and example configurations.

judge_gestalt.reason: Ship as-is. Production quality codebase with robust documentation and excellent modularity.

feature_completeness.reason: Full feature implementation including proximity, fuzzy matching, queries, serialization, and correct mutations.

correctness_edge_cases.reason: Handles long docs, missing fields correctly, but the fuzzy query does not properly limit scans and might be slow with many unique terms.

ranking_quality.reason: BM25 with smoothing, handles proximity bonus. It sums BM25 correctly but phrase scoring uses average distances rather than tight clusters.

type_safety_api_design.reason: Good type definitions, fully strictly typed, but single file is a bit monolithic compared to A.

performance.reason: Slow fuzzy search (p50 ~33ms, p99 193ms) and single-term queries lag behind the 500us target (p50 ~1.2ms).

test_evidence.reason: Good 51 tests across index building, query handling, and snippet logic.

code_quality_minimality.reason: All logic bundled together in a couple files, causing some readability issues, but functional and dependency-free.

readme_quality.reason: Clear and detailed README with architecture explanations, albeit slightly less structured than A.

judge_gestalt.reason: Very good, but slower fuzzy matching and lack of process logs pulls it back slightly.

feature_completeness.reason: Supports everything asked for in a single file implementation.

correctness_edge_cases.reason: Passes all tests but implementation relies on exhaustive iteration across inverted keys which leads to scaling issues.

ranking_quality.reason: Basic BM25 but proximity multiplier is a bit simplistic and is recalculated exhaustively.

type_safety_api_design.reason: Type signatures are solid, using generic Doc type.

performance.reason: Very poor performance across the board. Single term queries take >8ms on p50, OR/AND queries take extremely long (p50 >13ms).

test_evidence.reason: Passes the test harness but only 13 tests, providing less confidence on deep edge cases than A or B.

verification_artifacts.reason: Has process notes but they are somewhat brief and boilerplate-like, indicating a more basic workflow.

code_quality_minimality.reason: A 691-line monolithic index.ts. Harder to navigate and test in isolation.

judge_gestalt.reason: Too slow to be viable in production (fails the performance targets massively), needs an architecture refactor.

feature_completeness.reason: Implements all requested queries, BM25, snippets, and boolean parser.

correctness_edge_cases.reason: Handles the necessary cases, passes the harness.

type_safety_api_design.reason: Generic Doc support, clear API boundaries.

performance.reason: Incredible single-term (p50 146µs) and phrase queries (p50 34µs), but fuzzy takes ~20ms p50.

test_evidence.reason: Only 10 custom tests; relies mostly on the shared machine checks for correctness validation.

code_quality_minimality.reason: Single large file (844 LOC) reduces code modularity but is efficiently written.

judge_gestalt.reason: Performance is excellent on non-fuzzy queries, but test coverage is barebones and lacks process logs.

cross_notes: Variant A stands out as the only truly production-ready module; it breaks the logic down into proper files, writes 62 edge-case tests, and keeps performance consistent. Variant D is incredibly fast for exact terms but lacks unit test depth and module splitting. Variant B is functional but suffers on fuzzy performance. Variant C is far too slow across all queries (likely due to naive evaluation loops).
